purpose: Define the fixed governance-facing root kernel that every independent service must expose while keeping runtime implementation fully local and allowing repository-wide scans during governance review.

notes: app and src are alternatives; at least one is usually present.
UI runtimes often keep browser automation helpers under tools.
The governance kernel does not force a package layout.
ops is recommended when runtime ownership includes batch or queue handling.

rules: The governance kernel is mandatory for independently linked services.
Governance is attached to the service through the governance/ surface only.
All independent services must expose orchestration and prompts as fixed public-readable execution surfaces.
Monitoring artifacts under governance/monitoring are mandatory for both new and existing linked services.
The governance protocol chain agent -> prompts -> orchestration -> architecture -> shell -> human and agent collaboration -> DTP -> governance verdict is mandatory for independently linked services.
The boundary seal entries governance/VERSION, governance/policies, governance/bin, and governance/plan.json are mandatory.
Active link kits under governance/links/active must carry auth.contract.json and approval.receipt.json before out-of-governance cleanup is reviewable.
Architecture and shell contract surfaces must remain public-readable under orchestration/ for governance review.
Agent system and collaboration ideation prompt surfaces must remain public-readable under prompts/ for protocol review.
The central governance kernel defines required filenames only; it does not generate or own service-local content.
Whole-repository read scans are allowed for linking, hygiene review, and release checks.
Implementation folders outside the required kernel remain service-local and unconstrained by central governance.
Temporary-link kits must live under governance/links/active while evidence and review payloads remain outside the active link kit.
